ddls:
CREATE TABLE "diagnostic_types" (
  "diag_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each diagnostic type */,
  "diag_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the diagnostic type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the diagnostic type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the diagnostic type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the diagnostic type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("diag_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error report */,
  "diag_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the diagnostic type the error belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the error */,
  "severity_id" INTEGER /* ID of the severity level of the error */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category of the error */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the type of error */,
  "debuggable" INTEGER /* Whether the error is debuggable or not */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Detailed error message */,
  "error_stack_trace" TEXT /* Error stack trace */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error report was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error report was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_diag_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("diag_type_id") REFERENCES diagnostic_types ("diag_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES error_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_severity_id FOREIGN KEY ("severity_id") REFERENCES severities ("severity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "severities" (
  "severity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each severity level */,
  "severity_name" TEXT /* Name of the severity level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the severity level */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the severity level was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the severity level was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("severity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the error type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the error type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* ID of the error report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the error report */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the error report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the error report */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user accessing the error report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was assigned the role */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user's role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* ID of the error report being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user commenting */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_comments_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* ID of the error report the attachment belongs to */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the attachment file */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the attachment file */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the attachment file */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the attachment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the attachment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_attachments_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id")
)
